title: Nrin Vakil
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=GFFA |image=[[File:Nrin_Vakil.jpg]] |name=Nrin Vakil |homeworld=[[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]][[Star Wars Handbook 1: X-Wing Rogue Squadron|''Star Wars Handbook'' 1: ''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'']] |birth= |death= |species=[[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]][[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 13|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 13]] |gender= |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=[[Koyi Komad]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]'' |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] **[[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]] *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] **[[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] *[[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]]''[[Fate of the Jedi: Outcast]]'' |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|To each species, its own. Anything else is ''debauchery''.|Nrin Vakil|''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: Requiem for a Rogue]]''}} '''Nrin Vakil''' was a [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who flew for [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. He joined the squadron after their [[Mission to Mrlsst|mission]] on [[Mrlsst/Legends|Mrlsst]]. Vakil saw himself as a knight, and wore a modified [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]] filled with salt [[Water/Legends|water]] to remind himself of home. ==Biography== ===Going Rogue=== {{Quote|The only hope this world has for peace and economic prosperity rests with the nobility and the priamsta. They have ruled this world for generations.''"
"''Yes, but at what cost?! Time and time again, the royal class has sold out its people's blood and sweat to the Empire! Harran and the PLB are Eiattu's only chance for the new age of freedom!|Nrin Vakil and Ibtisam argue over their mission to Eiattu 6|[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 15|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 15]]}} [[File:NrinVakil.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Nrin Vakil.]] Nrin Vakil was a [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]]. He had joined the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Rebel Alliance Starfighter Corps]][[X-Wing: Rogue Leader 3|''X-Wing: Rogue Leader'' 3]] by [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' shortly after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].''[[Star Wars Omnibus: X-Wing Rogue Squadron Volume 1]]'' At the [[Time/Legends|time]], Vakil fancied himself a knight, and the Alliance his displaced and exiled [[Lord/Legends|lord]]. When [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] returned to the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] after a [[Raid at Coronet|mission]] to [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], Vakil was one of many [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilots]] who waited aboard a captured [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] for him to brief them, having volunteered to join the fabled [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance's]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] led the briefing until Antilles and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] joined them. Feeling he needed to "stretch his wings," Antilles ordered the recruits to get to their [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. Vakil officially joined the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] Rogue [[Squadron/Legends|Squadron]] approximately seven [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after [[Endor/Legends|Endor]].''[[Star Wars Omnibus: X-Wing Rogue Squadron Volume 2]]'' His [[Rebel flight suit|flight suit]] was a customized one that contained a [[Jumpsuit/Legends|coverall]] bladder of pressurized [[Water/Legends|saltwater]]. Intially, his Quarren heritage brought him into conflict with his [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] squadronmate [[Ibtisam]], but the two held their acrimony in check by order of their Rogue Leader. Shortly after their induction into the squadron, both Vakil and Ibtisam were taking part in [[Flight simulator/Legends|holosimulators]] [[Standard day/Legends|daily]], with fellow Rogue [[Feylis Ardele]] flying against all of her fellows in a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]]. During one simulation, Antilles also had the Rogue [[Herian I'ngre]] rig the pilots' seats with electrical charges in a bid to produce more realism. Vakil found the sensation exhilarating. As the Rogues unwound after the simulations, Vakil and Ibtisam argued over the future of [[Starfighter combat/Legends|starfighter combat]], with the Quarren going to bat for the classic [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]]. The debate was interrupted by the arrival of [[Grand Duke]] [[Gror Pernon]] of [[Eiattu 6]], who revealed that the Rogue pilot Plour Ilo was really [[Isplourrdacartha Estillo]], the lost [[Princess/Legends|princess]] of his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. Rogue Squadron soon embarked on their next [[Eiattu Campaign|mission]]: escort Ilo home, where it was hoped she could talk her [[Harrandatha Estillo|brother]] out of [[People's Liberation Battalion|rebelling]] against Eiattu 6's [[Priamsta|ruling nobles]] in the [[Eiattu Civil War]]. On the surface, a group of the Rogues explored some [[Jungle/Legends|jungles]] on the backs of native [[Thuvasaur]]s, with Vakil donning a traditional Eiattu [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] [[Hat/Legends|hat]].[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 14|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 14]] In a sudden [[Lightfight|firefight]] with [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], Vakil was determined to avenge the death of his steed, and he got even angrier when a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolt destroyed his headgear. Air support arrived from Antilles and [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]], but an enraged and careless Vakil narrowly avoided the explosion from a [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] [[Snubfighter/Legends|fighter]] that his Rogue Leader took out. The arrival of the Eiattu rebels finished off the Imperial [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]], and the Rogues were invited to the rebel base. There, Vakil maintained an aloofness while his squadron mates found themselves taken in by Ilo's charismatic brother. [[File:Brashnrin.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Vakil's carelessness nearly leads to his doom on Eiattu 6.]] In the planet's [[Eiattu 6's capital city|capital city]], Ilo also declared her support for the rebels, but when the rest of the Rogues returned from the jungle, Vakil voiced his disagreement. He argued with Ibtisam, insisting that Eiattu 6 owed its peace and prosperity to the [[Noble/Legends|nobles]], while his interlocutor asserted that they had been selling their people out to the Empire. Vakil received some support from I'ngre, but in the end, the Rogues shifted their focus to finding out more about the Imperial presence onworld. Armed with [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]], they returned to the jungle. There, Vakil found an Imperial [[gray noise generator]], used to mask the noise of large machines. He was caught in another explosion when it blew up, and when his fellows were captured by Imperial [[Scout trooper/Legends|biker scouts]], he was left to die in a large puddle of swamp water. After Vakil recovered his wits, he remained underwater—easily able to breathe it—and emerged when an Imperial [[Transport/Legends|transport]] returned to the scene to locate his body. He surprised and knocked [[Sleep/Legends|unconscious]] the [[Devers (Imperial)|two]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] aboard the transport, used its guns to take out nearby biker [[Scout/Legends|scouts]], and set off to rescue his comrades.[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 16|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 16]] Vakil arrived at an Imperial [[Prison/Legends|prison]] and disposed of the stationed [[Troop/Legends|troops]]. He set his companions free, and even received a hug from Ibtisam, but the Rogues's blaster bolts were unable to stop [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Leonia Tavira]] from fleeing the planet in an [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|Imperial shuttle]]. As it turned out, Ilo's brother was dead, and the Moff had replaced him with an unwitting [[Harrandatha Estillo (impostor)|impostor]]. Ilo exposed the charlatan at the [[Imperial Citadel (Eiattu 6)|Imperial citadel]] while Vakil was rescuing his comrades, and while Rogue Squadron prepared for their departure, the princess chose to remain onworld to help Eiattu 6 rebuild. ===Further missions=== {{Quote|I feel no rift between us. I feel very close to you."
"And I, you.|Ibtisam and Nrin Vakil, discussing each other|''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: Mandatory Retirement]]''}} Vakil maintained his aloofness with all his squadmates, and he liked to complain. During a [[Malrev incident|mission]] on [[Malrev IV]], it appeared he had a panic attack during a [[Ground warfare/Legends|ground battle]] with the native [[Irrukiine]], but he was actually rushing back towards his [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] to use it as an anti-personnel weapon. This caused Ibtisam to begin to re-evaluate her feelings towards Vakil. During that particular mission, members of the squadron like Celchu and Ibtisam had strange dreams, influenced by the [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] adept [[Cartariun]]. Ibtisam dreamed of Vakil addressing a crowd on [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]] where he was blaming the Mon Calamari for the woes on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. Ibtisam dreamed that she could influence his mind and played a practical joke on him. When recounting the dream to the [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] squadron member, Herian I'ngre, she remarked that Vakil seemed so passionate and eloquent, which appeared to further pique her interest. The [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Galactic Civil War)|campaign]] on [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]] saw Ibitsam shot down, which forced Vakil to face up to his feelings for his comrade. Her return to the squadron after being rescued by [[Kapp Dendo]] saw the two take the first steps towards a relationship. However, their relationship was not to be, as during a [[Evacuation of Ciutric|mission]] to rescue [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]], Ibtisam was shot down and [[Death/Legends|killed]] in action. During the evacuation, Pestage was obliged to share a shuttle with Ibtisam's body but he refused. Vakil overheard this and became incensed, attacking the former Imperial regent. He was pulled off by his fellow [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] and Pestage realized that he would not really have a place in the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. He sought refuge with [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Delak Krennel]] in the [[Ciutric Hegemony]], who strangled him to death. ===Thrawn campaign=== Saddened by Ibtisam's death and wanting to think things over, Vakil requested a leave of absence, followed by a transfer from Rogue Squadron and spent time training new recruits. However, the threat posed by the Empire, [[Thrawn campaign|newly reinvigorated]] under the leadership of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], led the New Republic to shift its assets about and Vakil resumed flying combat missions. During this time, Nrin was promoted through the ranks, and was made a [[Major/Legends|major]]''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'' by [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]].''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'' to [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]. Once Thrawn was [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|defeated]], the New Republic decided to target the breakaway [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] as they did not have the protection of the central Empire, and it was predicted that they would fall in short order. During the [[Hegemony theater|campaign]] against Delak Krennel, it became apparent that something big was happening in the Ciutric Hegemony. Information about the Hegemony was somewhat lacking, so it was decided that Rogue Squadron would conduct a number of reconnaissance missions. Vakil returned to the squadron to pilot a [[T-65BR X-wing reconnaissance starfighter]] otherwise known as a "snoopscoot". He survived the [[Battle of Distna|trap]] at [[Corvis Minor system/Legends|Corvis Minor]] and helped in the [[Battle of Ciutric|liberation]] of the [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] from the [[Lusankya/Legends|''Lusankya'']]. Celchu recognized his [[Commando/Legends|commando]] abilities and sent him with [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] and [[Ooryl Qrygg]] to free the prisoners from [[Ciutric IV]]. ===Later life=== He later [[Wedding of Nrin Vakil and Koyi Komad|married]] [[Koyi Komad]], the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] chief [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] for Rogue Squadron. In [[43 ABY]], he was part of a [[Mission to save Kessel|mission]] to save the [[Calrissian-Nunb Mines]] on [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]] from a series of destructive [[Groundquake/Legends|groundquakes]]. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Oddly enough, Plourr had originally been slated to be a Quarren, but Mike Baron missed that factoid in the original outline, so human she became. This is why, however, in "The Warrior Princess," one of the new pilots is a Quarren—I needed to get one in there somehow.|Michael A. Stackpole|}} [[Michael A. Stackpole]], creator of the ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron]]'' comic series, originally intended for the [[Human/Legends|Human]] character of Plour Ilo to be a Quarren in his outline for the first story arc, [[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: The Rebel Opposition|''The Rebel Opposition'']]. However, that fact was missed by the story's writer, [[Mike Baron]]. Accordingly, Stackpole created a new Quarren character—Nrin Vakil—for the later story arc [[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: The Warrior Princess|''The Warrior Princess'']]. Following his introduction, Vakil appeared in nearly every issue of ''Rogue Squadron'' until its conclusion, and he was one of several characters from the series to later receive a [[Hasbro]] 3¾ inch action figure.{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Legacy Collection|packtype=''Star Wars'' Comic Pack|pack=Ibtisam & Nrin Vakil (''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' #19)|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/TLCcomicpacksWM091.asp}} In the [[2008]] novel ''[[Luke Skywalker and the Shadows of Mindor]]'', Vakil is mentioned once but is mistakenly called "Nin."''[[Luke
